name: Chicken Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. In a kettle, combine the fowl, neck, and giblets with 12 cups cold water. Bring to a boil and skim any froth.
2. Add half a cup of cold water, then bring the stock to a simmer and skim any remaining froth.
3. Add the chopped onion, leeks, carrots, celery, salt, parsley sprigs, garlic clove, and bay leaf. Simmer the stock for 2 hours, skimming the froth periodically.
4. Remove the fowl from the kettle and set it aside. Remove the meat and skin from the carcass and reserve it for another use.
5. Chop the remaining carcass and return it to the kettle along with the skin. Continue simmering the stock, adding boiling water if needed to keep ingredients barely covered, for an additional 2 hours.
6. Strain the stock through a fine sieve into a bowl, pressing hard on the solids to extract all liquid. Allow the stock to cool.
7. Chill the stock and remove any remaining fat. The stock can be frozen for later use. This recipe makes approximately 6 cups of stock.
